explicit_text: I work as the laboratory manager at Clark Fork Valley Hospital in Plains, Montana. During the COVID-19 pandemic, our hospital was very proactive, even starting a respiratory infection clinic before some larger cities nearby. Being a smaller hospital, we had some advantages in adapting quickly, but also faced challenges like supply shortages as resources were prioritized for bigger facilities. Overall, the support from our administration and providers at Clark Fork Valley Hospital was strong throughout the crisis.

explicit_text: I live and work in Plains, Montana, where Clark Fork Valley Hospital is located. Being in a rural community here meant that COVID-19 cases were slow to start but eventually ramped up. Our small-town setting allowed us to adapt protocols quickly, but we also felt the impact of resource bottlenecks since larger population centers were prioritized for supplies. Despite that, we were able to prepare well and provide necessary care to our community during the pandemic.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic was an intense period where my laboratory team and I had to constantly adjust protocols and procedures, sometimes daily. We faced staffing shortages due to illness and supply challenges as resources were diverted to larger facilities. Despite the difficulties, we stayed proactive, starting a respiratory infection clinic early and maintaining rigorous safety measures. It was a confusing and stressful time, but communication and teamwork helped us manage the crisis effectively.

explicit_text: My interest in working in laboratories began during my time at the University of Wyoming. I was always more drawn to the hands-on laboratory work and science projects rather than just the academic side of things. That experience really shaped my career path and led me to become a laboratory manager later on. The University of Wyoming was where I first realized my passion for lab work.

entity: Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services
explicit_text: During the pandemic, the Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services was one of the key decision makers guiding our laboratory's protocols and procedures. We had to stay in close contact with them to ensure compliance with the latest guidelines. Their updates, along with those from the CDC, were something I referenced daily to keep our hospital aligned with state and national public health standards.

entity: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
explicit_text: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention played a crucial role during the COVID-19 pandemic by providing guidelines that our hospital laboratory had to follow. I referenced CDC updates daily to adjust our testing methods and safety protocols as the situation evolved. Their guidance helped us stay compliant and informed throughout the crisis, which was essential for protecting both staff and patients.
